title: HaorFloodAlert: Deseasonalized ML Ensemble for 72-Hour Flood Prediction in Bangladesh Haor Wetlands
core_claim: A deseasonalized machine-learning ensemble combining RF and XGBoost can forecast 72-hour flood probability for Bangladesh's Sunamganj haor wetlands, using Sentinel-1 SAR proxy signals and an operational alert pipeline.
triage_rationale: This looks application-focused but potentially useful: it claims a concrete operational setting, a nontrivial lead time, and an attempt to address seasonal leakage rather than just optimize accuracy. The abstract is still dominated by single-dataset performance claims and implementation details, so it is better queued for review than advanced immediately.
likely_red_flags: Very high performance claims on a small-looking event count (77 Sentinel-1 events)
Possible leakage/overfitting risk in a geographically and temporally narrow dataset
Abstract is metric-heavy but light on validation protocol details
Operational claims may not generalize beyond the Sunamganj haor context
